meta.title: Roman Catholic View of Justification (Part 1): Luther and the Reformation
meta.author: R. C. Sproul / Ligonier Ministries

questions.question: According to the lecture, Luther described justification by faith alone as:
options: A secondary issue compared to church governance
The article upon which the church stands or falls
Primarily a debate about Marian dogmas
A pragmatic compromise for political peace
questions.answer: The article upon which the church stands or falls

questions.question: Luther thanked Erasmus for addressing:
options: The heart issue: how a sinner finds salvation in Christ
The political structure of the Holy Roman Empire
questions.answer: The heart issue: how a sinner finds salvation in Christ

questions.question: The Latin verb justificare is said to have originally meant:
options: To declare righteous in a forensic hearing
questions.answer: To make righteous

questions.question: In the Roman Catholic development summarized, justification is placed:
options: Only at the moment of death
questions.answer: After sanctification (once righteousness is exhibited)

questions.question: The Protestant Reformation emphasis, drawing on Greek study, characterized justification more as:
options: To infuse substance into the soul
questions.answer: To declare righteous

questions.question: In the lecture's summary, Protestant ordering has justification:
questions.answer: Before sanctification

questions.question: Roman Catholic justification is described as beginning especially through:
questions.answer: The sacrament of baptism

questions.question: Ex opere operato is translated in the lecture as:
options: Through the working of the work
questions.answer: Through the working of the work

questions.question: Roman Catholic theology is said to resist describing baptism as:
options: Automatic if the recipient is hostile to the sacrament
Automatic in the sense Protestants sometimes infer from ex opere operato
questions.answer: Automatic in the sense Protestants sometimes infer from ex opere operato

questions.question: In baptism, Rome is said to teach that grace is:
questions.answer: Infused into the soul

questions.question: Protestants more commonly describe grace as:
options: A measurable spiritual substance stored in the soul
Human moral progress without divine aid
questions.answer: God's unmerited benevolence toward sinners

questions.question: The Council of Trent is cited for defining the need to:
options: Cooperate with and assent to the grace bestowed in baptism
Deny faith as necessary for justification
questions.answer: Cooperate with and assent to the grace bestowed in baptism

questions.question: According to the lecture, baptismal justification in this Roman framework is:
options: Not immutable; it can be lost
Purely symbolic with no status change
questions.answer: Not immutable; it can be lost

questions.question: Mortal sin is called mortal because it:
options: Is only a venial mistake about calendar dates
Is serious enough to cause the death of justifying grace infused at baptism
Is only concerned with financial restitution
questions.answer: Is serious enough to cause the death of justifying grace infused at baptism

questions.question: The drinking example used contrasts:
options: Wine vs beer as moral categories
Monastic vs lay drinking rules only
questions.answer: Tipsy (venial) vs drunk (mortal)

questions.question: Calvin is summarized as teaching that all sin is mortal in the sense that:
options: All sin is venial if confessed weekly
No sin deserves death after baptism
questions.answer: All sin deserves death

questions.question: Calvin is summarized as denying that any sin is mortal in the sense that:
options: It destroys saving grace received at justification
questions.answer: It destroys saving grace received at justification

questions.question: The sacrament of penance is called (in a cited 16th-century definition):
options: The first plank of justification for the baptized
The second plank of justification for those who made shipwreck of their faith
The sole means of initial justification
A replacement for baptism for converts
questions.answer: The second plank of justification for those who made shipwreck of their faith

questions.question: Priestly absolution is associated with the Latin phrase:
questions.answer: Te absolvo

questions.question: The lecture says Lutherans retained confession because:
options: They denied any need for assurance of pardon
They believed it salutary to confess sins to another with protected disclosure and ministerial assurance
They required congruous merit for each sin
They rejected New Testament mutual confession
questions.answer: They believed it salutary to confess sins to another with protected disclosure and ministerial assurance

questions.question: The speaker argues Rome affirms justification requires:
options: Faith, grace, and Christ--while the debate turned on 'alone' and added pluses
Neither faith nor grace if works exist
questions.answer: Faith, grace, and Christ--while the debate turned on 'alone' and added pluses

questions.question: The deeper Protestant concern with penance is identified as:
options: Works of satisfaction required to be restored to a state of grace
The use of Latin in worship
questions.answer: Works of satisfaction required to be restored to a state of grace

questions.question: Condign merit is described as merit that:
options: Is merely fitting but not real
Demands a reward such that God would be unjust not to reward it
questions.answer: Demands a reward such that God would be unjust not to reward it

questions.question: Works of satisfaction in penance are said to exemplify:
questions.answer: Meritum de congruo (congruous merit)

questions.question: Luther's view of justification by faith alone is described as a thunderbolt against:
options: Any notion that human works add to Christ's sole satisfaction for sin
Reading the New Testament in Greek
questions.answer: Any notion that human works add to Christ's sole satisfaction for sin
